title: Knowing God as Father part 3
scripture_text: ‘’Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven. So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honoured by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full, but when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you pray, go into your room, close the door and pray to your Father, who is unseen. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not keep on babbling like pagans, for they think they will be heard because of their many words. Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him. This, then, is how you should pray: “‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today our daily bread. And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors., And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For if you forgive other people when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you. But if you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins. “When you fast, do not look sombre as the hypocrites do, for they disfigure their faces to show others they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face, so that it will not be obvious to others that you are fasting, but only to your Father, who is unseen; and your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy and where thieves break in and steal, But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven where moths and vermin do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal. For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are healthy, your whole body will be full of light. But if your eyes are unhealthy, our whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness! “No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? And why do you worry about clothes? See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labour or spin. Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendour was dressed like one of these. If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith? So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear. For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them. But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own’’
devotional:
What can we learn about God being our Father from this chapter?

1. The spiritual disciplines we practice must be done in the context of enjoying God as father.

It is so easy to reduce our relationship with God into a religious box-ticking exercise where reputation becomes more important than intimacy. These people became religious, doing the right things in the wrong way, and gradually they became trapped in a need to get their affirmation and approval from others because they had not grasped that God is their father. When people loose their way spiritually it is not because they are bad people, but their waywardness can be traced back to the fact that they have ceased to believe that God is a loving father.

2. Because God is our Father, we need to be real and honest before him.

When we are told that we should do our acts of righteousness in secret, it would be very easy to put a literal translation on this and think you should only pray in a private place, but that misses the point. Someone could do something in secret and still have a heart of a hypocrite. The secret refers to an attitude that is the antithesis to that of the hypocrite.

Sometimes we act before God in the same way the hypocrites comes before men. They acted in a certain way to get the approval and praise of men. They acted out of unreality in order to impress others; they were not being real or honest.

Sometimes that is how we come before God, we think we need to impress him, we need to win his approval, we are not honest about ourselves, and we have the same heart attitudes as the hypocrite who engages in a public performance. Our father is looking for authenticity not performance.

I should also mention that we all have a love-inspired need from time to time to shut the door to the world and everything else and be alone with God because we love him and long to give ourselves to him.

3. As our Father, God wants to enrich our lives.

There is a very interesting play on words that we don’t pick up in our English translations. The word that is used for the reward that the hypocrite receives from other people, and the word for reward that is used to describe the father’s response to authentic spirituality are different. The reward of the hypocrite is misthos which was used to describe a persons wage or salary, it is the same word used to describe a hired hand. Misthos describes the heart of the taker. These people thought in terms of deserving something, they had a ‘You owe me’ mindset. They treat God like an employer who owes them something for their good behaviour.

The other word apodidomi reflects the heart of the giver. When the father rewards his children it is his deep expression of his pleasure and delight, the father finds great joy and satisfaction. The rewards that the father gives his children are to teach us about his heart. They are a deep expression of God’s satisfaction and pleasure. The reward is the Joy of God’s heart flowing over into ours. Knowing God as Father will change our understanding of prayer.

Tim Keller says “One of the most basic things that the gospel does is change prayer from mere petition to fellowship and the praise of his glory.” https://gospelcenteredmusings.com/2008/05/19/tim-keller-the-prayer-and-the-gospel/

Accessed 05/05/2025

Understanding prayer as a means of fellowship with the father and a way of developing and maturing our relationship with him will transform how and why we pray. Authentic fellowship with God our father will cause the reorientation of our desires, hopes and dreams. We will be anxious to honour his name, we will want his kingdom to come, and we will desire to commit our lives to his will, because we understand that he is our father.

Knowing God as your Father will help you establish healthy priorities and motivations for living. Reading through Matthew 6 can be very daunting. Think of the kind of things Jesus asks of us in this chapter.

We are to hallow Gods name

Pray that his kingdom will come

Ensure that his will be done

Forgive those who have hurt us

Live a life of dependence

Endeavour to avoid sin

Make sure that our treasure is in heaven not on earth

Avoid all worry,

Seek desperately for righteousness.

The important thing to understand is that guilt and condemnation cannot help or encourage us to do any of the above.

This kind of living is only possible as we discover that God really is our Father and He wants us to enjoy being his children.
prayer: Father thank you that your enjoyment of us is expressed by how you reward us.

Knowing God as Father part 3

Study Text: Matthew 6
‘’Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven. So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honoured by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full, but when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you pray, go into your room, close the door and pray to your Father, who is unseen. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not keep on babbling like pagans, for they think they will be heard because of their many words. Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him. This, then, is how you should pray: “‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today our daily bread. And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors., And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For if you forgive other people when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you. But if you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins. “When you fast, do not look sombre as the hypocrites do, for they disfigure their faces to show others they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are healthy, your whole body will be full of light. But if your eyes are unhealthy, our whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness! “No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them Are you not much more valuable than they? But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own’’

These people became religious, doing the right things in the wrong way, and gradually they became trapped in a need to get their affirmation and approval from others because they had not grasped that God is their father. When people loose their way spiritually it is not because they are bad people, but their waywardness can be traced back to the fact that they have ceased to believe that God is a loving father.

Sometimes we act before God in the same way the hypocrites comes before men. They acted in a certain way to get the approval and praise of men. They acted out of unreality in order to impress others; they were not being real or honest.

The other word apodidomi reflects the heart of the giver. When the father rewards his children it is his deep expression of his pleasure and delight, the father finds great joy and satisfaction. The rewards that the father gives his children are to teach us about his heart. They are a deep expression of God’s satisfaction and pleasure. Tim Keller says “One of the most basic things that the gospel does is change prayer from mere petition to fellowship and the praise of his glory.” https://gospelcenteredmusings.com/2008/05/19/tim-keller-the-prayer-and-the-gospel/

Accessed 05/05/2025

Understanding prayer as a means of fellowship with the father and a way of developing and maturing our relationship with him will transform how and why we pray. Authentic fellowship with God our father will cause the reorientation of our desires, hopes and dreams. The important thing to understand is that guilt and condemnation cannot help or encourage us to do any of the above.

Prayer: Father thank you that your enjoyment of us is expressed by how you reward us.
Bible in a year reading plan: - 1 Chronicles 1-3
